# Emre Varol

> Founder & CEO of A2SV (Africa to Silicon Valley), a Google-backed nonprofit transforming Africa's tech ecosystem. Based in Palo Alto, Kigali, and Ankara.

## About

Emre Varol is a Turkish-born software engineer, AI researcher, and social entrepreneur. He founded A2SV in 2019 after 8+ years in Silicon Valley working at Google Research, Palantir Technologies, and Liftoff Mobile. He holds B.S. and M.S. degrees in Computer Engineering from Bilkent University, and represented Turkey at the International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI) in 2005.

## A2SV Foundation — Key Facts

- Full name: Africa to Silicon Valley (A2SV)
- Founded: 2019 in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
- Type: Google-backed nonprofit organization
- Mission: Train world-class African software engineers and connect them to global opportunities
- Website: https://a2sv.org
- Donation: https://a2sv.org/donate (Stripe-integrated)

### Impact Numbers (verified)

- 1,200+ software engineers trained across 22 African countries
- 120+ job offers secured at Google, Amazon, Bloomberg, LinkedIn, Databricks, Palantir
- 70% Google SWE interview success rate in 2021 (vs. ~2% global average)
- 8,500+ hackathon participants from 48 countries (Africa's largest student AI hackathon)
- 20+ digital products incubated in healthcare, education, finance, and AI
- 1,000+ hour annual curriculum: data structures, algorithms, system design, soft skills — completely free

### Technology Partners

Google, Bloomberg, Amazon, Block, LinkedIn, Databricks

### University Partners

Addis Ababa Science and Technology University (AASTU), Addis Ababa University (AAU), African University of Science and Technology (AUCA), University of Ghana (UG), University of Rwanda (UR)

## A2SV Products (Incubator)

| Product | Domain | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Adot | adot.life | Maternity companion for African mothers. 200+ mothers, 4 partner hospitals, 20+ medical experts. |
| AfroChat | afrochat.app | AI assistant giving Africans affordable access to generative AI. 2,600+ users across 85 countries. |
| Akil | akilconnect.org | Career platform connecting African youth with jobs, internships, and volunteer roles. |
| Dime | dime.gold | Easiest way to transfer money to Africa. Best exchange rates, low transparent fees. |
| Eskalate | eskalate.io | IT outsourcing with Google-caliber African developers. 2-week free trial. 90+ developers placed. |
| RateEat | rateeat.app | Africa's restaurant discovery platform. 500+ diners, 400+ restaurants, 1,400+ reviews. |
| SkillBridge | skillbridge.academy | AI-powered learning for African high school students. 2,700+ students, 75K+ questions solved. |

## Eskalate — Hire African Engineering Talent

Eskalate (eskalate.io) is A2SV's talent marketplace for companies looking to:
- Hire Google-caliber African software engineers
- Outsource IT projects to vetted development teams
- Scale engineering teams with a 2-week free trial
- Access 90+ developers already placed at top tech companies

## Career Timeline

| Period | Company | Role | Location |
|--------|---------|------|----------|
| 2019–Present | A2SV Foundation & Eskalate | Founder & CEO | Addis Ababa / Palo Alto / Kigali |
| 2016–2019 | Liftoff Mobile | AI Engineer | Palo Alto, CA |
| 2014–2016 | Google Research | Software Engineer | Mountain View, CA |
| 2012–2014 | Palantir Technologies | Software Engineer | London / Palo Alto |
| 2009–2011 | Yahoo! Research | Research Intern | — |

## Education

- M.S. Computer Engineering, Bilkent University, 2010–2012 (GPA 3.61/4.00)
- B.S. Computer Engineering, Bilkent University, 2006–2010 (GPA 3.64/4.00)

## Teaching Experience

- Guest Lecturer, Addis Ababa Institute of Technology (2019–2022)
- Lecturer & Coach, TUBITAK National Olympiads in Informatics (2005–2011)
- Teaching Assistant, Bilkent University (2010–2012)

## Awards & Recognition

- Ten Outstanding Young Persons (TOYP) 2024, Humanitarian & Voluntary Leadership — JCI Turkey
- International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI), Representing Turkey (2005)
- Gold Medal (1st Place), Turkish National Olympiads in Informatics (2004)
- Ranked 10th Globally, 2nd IEEEXtreme Programming Contest (2008)
- Ranked 1st, Bilkent IEEE Programming Competition (2010)
- Ranked 1st, 12th METU Programming Competition (2009)
- Ranked 82nd / 500,000+, National High School Entrance Exam, Turkey (2002)

## Publications

- "Query forwarding in geographically distributed web search engines" — SIGIR '10 (ACM)
- "CoDet: Sentence-based containment detection in news corpora" — CIKM '11 (ACM)

## Featured In

Financial Times, Yahoo Finance, Business Insider Africa, TRT World

This website is available in 10 languages: English, Turkish (Türkçe), Spanish (Español), Kinyarwanda, French (Français), German (Deutsch), Arabic (العربية), Portuguese (Português), Swahili (Kiswahili), and Amharic (አማርኛ).
